description: Grow your own snack-size tomatoes on a windowsill or balcony. Cherry varieties are the easiest for indoor growing — compact, productive, and ripen fast. About 60-70 days from planting to first harvest.

care.water_frequency: Every 2-3 days in summer, daily in hot weather. Consistent watering prevents cracking.
care.water_winter: Not applicable — annual warm-season plant. Start new seeds in spring.
care.light_preferred: Full sun 8+ hours daily. South-facing window minimum; balcony or grow light ideal.
care.light_also_ok: Bright indirect with supplemental grow light (at least 14 hours total light)
care.humidity_action: Good air circulation prevents fungal disease. Avoid wetting leaves when watering.
care.soil_types: Rich, well-draining potting mix with compost. At least a 5-liter pot for bush varieties.
care.repot_frequency: Annual plant. Pot up seedlings once when they outgrow starter pots.
care.fertilizer_type: Tomato-specific fertilizer (high potassium) weekly once flowering starts. Calcium supplement prevents blossom end rot.
care.difficulty: Medium — needs lots of light and consistent feeding, but very rewarding. Bush varieties are easier indoors.
care.watering_guide: Water deeply until water drains from the bottom. Consistent moisture is key — fluctuations cause fruit cracking and blossom end rot. Mulch the surface to retain moisture.
care.light_guide: The single most important factor. If seedlings are leggy and pale, they need more light. A grow light is almost essential for indoor growing without a south-facing window or balcony.
care.tips: Choose bush/determinate varieties for indoors: Tiny Tim, Red Robin, Micro Tom. Hand-pollinate by gently shaking the plant or tapping flowers when they open. Pinch off suckers to focus energy on fruit. Harvest when fully colored.
care.toxicity_note: Ripe fruit is safe. Leaves, stems, and green unripe fruit contain solanine — mildly toxic to pets and small children if eaten in quantity.
common_problems: Blossom end rot (black spot on bottom of fruit — calcium deficiency from inconsistent watering)
Leggy seedlings (not enough light — the #1 indoor problem, get a grow light)
Blossom drop / no fruit (too hot, too cold, or not pollinated — shake the plant daily during flowering)
common_pests: Aphids (green clusters on stems — blast with water or insecticidal soap)
Whiteflies (tiny white moths — yellow sticky traps)
Spider mites (in dry indoor air — mist around plant, not on leaves)
